title: 10 Soothing Herbal Teas for Eye Twitching and Relaxation
intro_desc: Herbal teas for eye twitching are natural remedies made from plants that help alleviate the involuntary muscle spasms causing twitching in the eyes. These teas work by promoting relaxation, reducing stress and anxiety, and soothing the nervous system. The best herbal teas for eye twitching include Passiflora incarnata, Valeriana officinalis, Lavandula angustifolia, Melissa officinalis, Avena sativa, Hypericum perforatum, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Cinchona officinalis, Achillea millefolium, and Zingiber officinale. If left untreated, eye twitching can lead to sleep disturbances, fatigue, and decreased productivity, negatively impacting daily life and overall well-being.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis tea, also known as valerian root tea, is a natural remedy that may help with eye twitching. Eye twitching, also known as blepharospasm, is a condition where the eyelid or the muscles around the eye twitch or spasm. This can be caused by stress, fatigue, or an imbalance in the body's nervous system. Valeriana officinalis tea has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to relax the muscles around the eye and reduce twitching. The active ingredients in valerian root, such as valerenic acid and valeranone, work to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation, which can help to reduce eye twitching. Additionally, valerian root tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and redness around the eye, making it a soothing and calming remedy for eye twitching. Drinking valerian root tea may also help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can be contributing factors to eye twitching.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Valerenic acid in Ziziphus Jujuba is good for eye twitching because it has a calming effect on the muscles around the eyes, reducing spasms and helping to relax the nervous system.
Valeranone is good for eye twitching because it helps to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common causes of eye twitching, by acting as a natural sedative and promoting relaxation.
Valerenal is good for eye twitching because it has a calming effect that can help to relax the muscles around the eyes, reducing the occurrence of twitching.
Tannins are good for eye twitching because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to soothe and calm the muscles around the eye, reducing spasms and twitching.
Valerenol is good for eye twitching because it has anti-anxiety and anti-inflammatory properties that help to calm the muscles around the eyes and reduce irritation.

remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal teas for eye twitching
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ziziphus jujuba herbal tea that helps with eye twitching, start by gathering dried jujube fruits or roots, depending on your preference. You can purchase these online or at a local health food store. Rinse the dried jujube with warm water to remove any impurities, then add one teaspoon of the dried jujube to a cup of boiling water. Let it steep for about 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors and medicinal properties to infuse into the water. Strain the tea into a separate cup and discard the solids. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but it's recommended to drink it plain to get the full benefits. Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally before meals, to help alleviate eye twitching and other symptoms. It's essential to note that jujube tea is not a substitute for medical treatment, and if you experience persistent or severe eye twitching, consult a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata tea, also known as passionflower tea, is a natural remedy that can help with eye twitching. Eye twitching, or blepharospasm, is a common condition where the eyelid muscles involuntarily contract and relax. This can be caused by stress, fatigue, caffeine, or certain medical conditions. Passiflora incarnata tea contains compounds that have a soothing effect on the nervous system and can help to calm the muscles in the face, including the eyelids. The tea has been traditionally used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness, all of which can contribute to eye twitching. By reducing stress and promoting relaxation, Passiflora incarnata tea may help to alleviate eye twitching. The flavonoids and alkaloids present in the tea may also help to reduce inflammation and spasms in the muscles, which can contribute to eye twitching. Drinking Passiflora incarnata tea regularly may help to provide relief from eye twitching and promote a sense of calm and relaxation.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Flavonoids are good for eye twitching because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help calm and relax the muscles around the eyes, reducing twitching and spasms naturally.
Polyphenols are good for eye twitching because they have strong antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and protect the eyes from damage, which can cause or worsen twitching.
Quercetin is good for eye twitching because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce muscle spasms and relax the eye muscles, providing relief from twitching and spasms.

Kaempferol is good for eye twitching because its bioactive properties help to relax and calm the muscles around the eyes, reducing spasms and twitching by promoting better blood flow and reducing inflammation.
Tannins may help with eye twitching because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and calm irritated nerves around the eyes, reducing twitching and spasms.

remedies.image_alt: Passiflora incarnata herbal teas for eye twitching
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ziziphus jujuba herbal tea that helps with eye twitching, start by gathering 1-2 teaspoons of dried Ziziphus jujuba fruit, also known as Chinese date or jujube. You can either buy it from a local health store or online, or grow it yourself if you have the climate suitable. Next, boil 1 cup of water in a teapot or kettle. Then, add the dried Ziziphus jujuba fruit to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the fruit to release its nutrients and flavors into the water. After the steeping time is up, strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth to remove the fruit, and discard the solids. You can then add honey or sugar to taste, if desired, and drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate eye twitching symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm tea, is a herbal remedy that can help with eye twitching. Eye twitching, or blepharospasm, is a common condition where the eyelid suddenly and uncontrollably twitches or spasms. The tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce muscle spasms in the eyes. Lemon balm tea also contains a compound called rosmarinic acid, which has antioxidant properties that can help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, the tea has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that may be contributing to eye twitching. Drinking lemon balm tea may also help relax the muscles around the eyes, reducing the frequency and severity of twitching episodes.

Apigenin is good for eye twitching because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and calm down the nerve endings in the eye muscles, thus providing relief from the twitching sensation.

remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal teas for eye twitching
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ziziphus jujuba herbal tea for eye twitching, start by gathering dried Jujube fruit, also known as Red Date or Chinese Date. Begin by boiling 1 cup of water in a pot, then reduce the heat to a simmer. Add 2 teaspoons of dried Jujube fruit to the pot, and let it steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavors to infuse into the water. Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the fruit pieces. Discard the solids and let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature before drinking. It's recommended to drink 1-2 cups of Jujube tea per day to help alleviate eye twitching and promote relaxation. You can also add honey or sugar to sweeten the tea if desired, but be sure to drink it on an empty stomach for better absorption of the herbal properties.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia tea, also known as lavender tea, is a natural remedy that may help with eye twitching. This tea is made from the flowers of the lavender plant, which have been used for centuries to calm the mind and body. The tea is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety that may be causing the eye twitching. Lavender tea also contains a compound called linalool, which has a calming effect on the nervous system. When consumed, it can help to relax the muscles and calm the nerves, reducing the spasms that cause eye twitching. Drinking lavender tea before bedtime may also help to promote a good night's sleep, which is essential for eye health and can help to reduce the frequency of eye twitching. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce puffiness and redness in the eyes, making it a soothing and calming remedy for eye twitching.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a soothing Ziziphus jujuba herbal tea that helps with eye twitching, start by gathering a handful of dried Ziziphus jujuba seeds or berries, which are commonly known as jujube or Chinese date. Rinse the seeds in a fine mesh strainer under cold running water, then place them in a heat-resistant cup or teapot. Pour 8-10 ounces of boiling water over the seeds and let them steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors and nutrients to infuse into the water. After the steeping time, strain the tea into another cup using a fine mesh strainer to catch the seeds, and discard the seeds. You can add honey or a slice of lemon to taste, but be sure to drink the tea while it's still warm, as the heat helps to release its medicinal properties. Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help calm eye twitching and reduce stress and anxiety, and remember to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or allergies.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tea, also known as ginger tea, is a natural remedy for eye twitching. The active compound in ginger, called gingerol, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe and calm the muscles around the eyes. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ziziphus jujuba herbal tea that helps with eye twitching, first, you need to gather the right ingredients. You will need dried Ziziphus jujuba seeds, a tea infuser or a piece of cheesecloth, and a cup. Start by boiling water in a kettle or on the stove. While the water is boiling, measure out one teaspoon of dried Ziziphus jujuba seeds and place them in the tea infuser or cheesecloth. Once the water has reached a boil, carefully pour it over the seeds in the infuser or cheesecloth. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, which will help release the active ingredients from the seeds into the water. After the steeping time has passed, carefully lift out the infuser or cheesecloth and discard the seeds. Your Ziziphus jujuba herbal tea is now ready to drink. To use it for eye twitching, try drinking 1-2 cups a day for a few days to see if it helps to reduce the twitching.

Begin by boiling one liter of water in a saucepan, then add one tablespoon of dried jujube fruit and flowers. Once the mixture has cooled, strain it into a separate container to remove the solids. You can store the tea in the refrigerator for up to three days. To consume, steep one teaspoon of the tea in a cup of hot water for three to five minutes, then strain and drink. It's recommended to drink one to two cups of jujube tea per day, ideally before meals or at bedtime, to help alleviate eye twitching and promote overall well-being.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ziziphus jujuba tea, also known as jujube tea, is a traditional remedy used to help relieve eye twitching. The main reason jujube tea is helpful for eye twitching is that it contains antioxidants and flavonoids that can reduce inflammation and calm the nerves. supplementary_best: A combination of herbal teas containing Valeriana officinalis, Melissa officinalis, and Avena sativa may help remedy eye twitching. Valeriana officinalis has a relaxing effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce anxiety and stress that may be contributing to the twitching. Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, has a calming effect and can help to soothe the mind and body. Avena sativa, or oat straw, is known to have a balancing effect on the nervous system and can help to reduce muscle tension, which may be causing the twitching. This combination provides a multi-faceted approach to addressing the underlying causes of eye twitching, addressing both the nervous system and muscle tension.
supplementary_avoid: If you have eye twitching, you may want to avoid some herbal teas that can affect your nervous system.  One such herb is 'Yerba Mate' (Ilex paraguariensis), which contains caffeine and can increase the activity of nerves in the body, including those that control eye movements. This may make eye twitching worse.  Another herb to be cautious with is 'Ginseng' (Panax ginseng), which is known to stimulate the nervous system. Ginseng can increase alertness and energy, but it can also cause muscle tension and spasms, including eye twitching.  You should also limit or avoid 'Peppermint' (Mentha piperita) tea, as it can cause your muscles to contract and relax rapidly, leading to eye twitching. This is because peppermint contains a compound called menthol, which can affect the nerves that control muscle movements.  Another herb to be careful with is 'Cayenne Pepper' (Capsicum annuum), which contains a compound called capsaicin. Capsaicin can cause your nerves to become overactive, leading to eye twitching and other muscle spasms.  Finally, you should also be cautious with 'Guarana' (Paullinia cupana) tea, as it contains high levels of caffeine, which can increase the activity of your nervous system and worsen eye twitching.  These herbal teas may interact with each other or with other medications, so it's best to talk to a healthcare professional before consuming them if you have eye twitching.
supplementary_other_remedies: Eye twitching can be caused by various factors such as fatigue, stress, or certain nutrients deficiencies. A warm compress applied to the eye area can help relax the muscles and reduce twitching. To make a warm compress, soak a clean cloth in warm water, wring it out, and apply it to the eye area for 5-7 minutes. The heat helps to increase blood flow and calm the muscles.  Another natural remedy is to massage the eye area gently. Use your ring finger to massage the area around the eye in a circular motion. This helps to relax the muscles and reduce tension. Some people also find relief by applying a small amount of castor oil or coconut oil to the eye area, as these oils can help to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles.  Eye twitching can also be caused by dehydration, so drinking plenty of water is essential. Another option is to try a neti pot or a saline rinse to help moisten the eyes and reduce twitching. Additionally, getting enough sleep and practicing stress-reducing techniques such as meditation or deep breathing can also help to reduce eye twitching.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who experience eye twitching may also have other issues. They might have headaches or migraines due to muscle strain and nerve irritation. This is because the same nerve that controls the eyelid muscle also controls other facial muscles. The herb feverfew has anti-inflammatory properties that can help ease these headaches.  Eye twitching can also be a sign of stress and anxiety, which can lead to insomnia. The herb valerian root has a calming effect that can help with sleep. It can also help with muscle relaxation and reduce anxiety.  Some people with eye twitching may also have dry eyes or irritation. This can be due to allergies or environmental factors. The herb eyebright has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the eyes.  Additionally, eye twitching can be a symptom of nutrient deficiencies, such as magnesium or potassium deficiencies. The herb dandelion root can help replenish these nutrients and ease eye twitching.  The herb passionflower can help with anxiety and stress-related eye twitching. It has a calming effect that can help reduce muscle tension.  The herb chamomile can also help with eye twitching caused by allergies or environmental factors. It has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the eyes and calm the nervous system.
